items.title: pdb
items.description: pdb pauses execution; logging tells you what happened after the fact. Both belong in your toolkit.

items.title: IDE Debuggers
items.description: Logpoints are IDE-only ephemeral logs; the `logging` module is the persistent, production-safe equivalent.

items.title: Error & Exception Handling
items.description: `logger.exception(...)` inside `except` is the idiomatic Python pattern for reporting failures.

items.title: Fixtures & Mocks
items.description: pytest's `caplog` fixture lets you assert that specific log lines were emitted.

items.title: Memory Profiling
items.description: When investigating a slow leak, structured logs of allocation milestones pair well with `tracemalloc` snapshots.
